one_liner: Vonage (developer.vonage.com) is a broad communications API platform (SMS, Voice, Messages, Verify, Video, Numbers, etc.) using API Key/Secret Basic Auth or JWT depending on product, with self-serve signup and trial credit, and an existing official MCP server offering.

auth_methods.notes: API Key and Secret Base64-encoded in Authorization header; simplest to get started but lacks webhook/ACL support for some APIs like Messages and Verify.
JSON Web Token (JWT) signed with application's private key, sent as Bearer token; recommended for production, supports webhooks and ACLs.
SIP Trunking uses Digest Authentication with API Key as user and API Secret as password.

self_serve_notes: Developers can sign up and get API key/secret immediately from the Vonage Dashboard with no credit card required; new accounts receive free trial credit for testing. No permanent free tier — must move to paid plan after trial credit is exhausted. Pricing available via Pricing API or dashboard.

api_surface.notes: Wide range of APIs: Accounts, Application, Audit, Conversation, Conversion, Device Location, Dispatch, Identity Insights, Media, Messages, Numbers, Number Insight, Pricing v1/v2, Programmable SIP, Quality on Demand, Redact, Reports, SMS, Verify (Legacy/v2), Voice, Video — each with documented REST endpoints and JWT/Basic auth.

evidence.supports: Details Basic Auth (API Key/Secret) and JWT authentication methods, varying by API product.
Confirms Basic Auth and JWT Bearer options for Verify API, with example requests and header format.
Describes self-serve onboarding and free trial credit for new users.
Confirms no permanent free tier; usage-based pricing after trial credit.
Example of REST API surface with JWT/Basic auth support for Messages API.

flags: Existing official MCP servers already provided by Vonage (documentation MCP + tooling/API-bindings MCP via npm package), reducing need to build from scratch.
No permanent free tier — developers rely on trial credit then must pay, which could affect self-serve sustainability for agent tooling testing.
Auth method varies per API product (Basic vs JWT vs Digest for SIP), requiring careful handling in a unified toolkit.
